journal_entry.description: Pledge of $1,000,000 donation to humanitarian food aid program in Somalia with 1-month promissory note
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, a donation pledge creates an expense (charitable contribution) when the commitment is made. The promissory note creates a liability (Notes Payable) that will be settled in one month. The debit tuple (0,0,1,0,0,0) correctly identifies this as an expense increase, and the credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) correctly identifies a liability increase. No tax implications for charitable donations.

output.reason: Target pledged a donation and issued a promissory note (liability). The debit side records the donation expense increase. The donation commitment creates an expense at the time of the pledge under IFRS, even though payment will occur later via the promissory note.

debit_classifier.reason: Target pledged a donation and issued a promissory note (liability). The debit side records the donation expense increase. The donation commitment creates an expense at the time of the pledge under IFRS, even though payment will occur later via the promissory note.

output.reason: Target pledges a donation and issues a promissory note. On the credit side: (1) The promissory note creates a liability increase (notes payable) for $1,000,000. This is a formal obligation to pay. The debit side would record the donation expense, but we only classify credits here.

credit_classifier.reason: Target pledges a donation and issues a promissory note. On the credit side: (1) The promissory note creates a liability increase (notes payable) for $1,000,000. This is a formal obligation to pay. The debit side would record the donation expense, but we only classify credits here.
